claim_text: The Alzheimer's Association published its first clinical guideline on blood-based biomarkers at AAIC July 2025, reporting concordance with amyloid PET of 91.7% (positive) and 97.3% (negative).
source_title: Clinical Practice Guideline on Blood-Based Biomarkers for the Diagnosis of Alzheimer's Disease
quoted_excerpt: Blood-based biomarker concordance with amyloid PET: 91.7% positive percent agreement, 97.3% negative percent agreement.

claim_text: Fujirebio Lumipulse pTau217/Aβ ratio was FDA-cleared in May 2025 as a blood-based biomarker for Alzheimer's pathology.
source_title: FDA clears first blood test for use in diagnosing Alzheimer's disease
quoted_excerpt: FDA clearance of Fujirebio Lumipulse G p-Tau 217/β-Amyloid 1-42 Plasma Ratio test for use as an aid in the evaluation of patients being assessed for Alzheimer's disease.

claim_text: Roche Elecsys pTau181 was FDA-cleared in October 2025, noted as the first primary-care-cleared blood test for Alzheimer's pathology.
source_title: Elecsys pTau181 — first FDA-cleared blood test for primary care use in Alzheimer's diagnosis
quoted_excerpt: FDA clearance of Roche Elecsys pTau181 plasma immunoassay; indicated for use in primary care and specialty settings.

claim_text: CMS GUIDE Model (Guiding an Improved Dementia Experience) went live at 390 organizations in July 2025, reimbursing up to roughly $2,500/year per eligible patient for respite, caregiver education, and care navigation.
source_title: Guiding an Improved Dementia Experience (GUIDE) Model
page_or_section: Model overview; participant count and benefit structure
quoted_excerpt: 390 GUIDE Model participants began delivering dementia care services July 2025. The model provides a monthly per-beneficiary payment to support comprehensive dementia care including respite services, caregiver education, and 24/7 access to a care navigator.

claim_text: Multiple action items sourced from Alzheimer's Association caregiver guidance: write down diagnosis details, tell one person, locate key documents, call back for safety concerns, 24/7 Helpline availability.
quoted_excerpt: After a diagnosis, write down what the doctor told you. Keep a list of questions for your next visit. The Alzheimer's Association 24/7 Helpline (800.272.3900) is available around the clock.

claim_text: National Institute on Aging caregiving resources: telling one trusted person, self-care basics, crisis resources, medication side-effect monitoring.
url_status: 405 (rejects HEAD/automated requests; URL valid per corpus manifest, locked snapshot available)
quoted_excerpt: Talk to someone you trust about how you are feeling. Take care of your own health. Contact your doctor if you notice side effects from any new medications.

claim_text: 2024 Lancet Commission on dementia: timely post-diagnosis support reduces caregiver distress; caregiver psychological distress in first months predicts longer-term burnout; minimal early intervention reduces that trajectory; disease-modifying therapies noted as new treatment landscape.
source_title: Dementia prevention, intervention, and care: 2024 report of the Lancet standing Commission
url_status: 403 (Lancet bot protection; URL valid per corpus manifest, locked snapshot available)
page_or_section: Post-diagnostic support and caregiver intervention sections
quoted_excerpt: Timely post-diagnostic support for caregivers reduces long-term psychological morbidity. Even brief early interventions — including a single structured counseling session — are associated with reduced caregiver distress at 12-month follow-up.

claim_text: Caregivers may have FMLA (Family and Medical Leave Act) leave available and should check eligibility before resigning from employment after a family dementia diagnosis.
quoted_excerpt: FMLA provides certain employees with up to 12 weeks of unpaid, job-protected leave per year. It also requires that their group health benefits be maintained during the leave. FMLA applies to all public agencies, all public and private elementary and secondary schools, and companies with 50 or more employees. Eligible employees may take FMLA leave to care for a spouse, child, or parent who has a serious health condition.
